title: Charles-Antoine Soulière
content:
## Charles-Antoine Soulière
Québec City Office Lead Partner | Life Sciences Sector Co-Leader
People.ContactEmail casouliere@mccarthy.ca
- Université de Sherbrooke
Recognized for his practical, business‑driven approach, Charles‑Antoine provides strategic, sector‑focused advice that helps clients seize opportunities, manage risk, and advance their most complex transactions.
Charles-Antoine Soulière is the Québec City Office Lead at McCarthy Tétrault, co-lead of the Firm’s Life Sciences Group and a partner in the Business Law Group. He advises clients on complex corporate and securities matters, with a focus on mergers and acquisitions, corporate finance, capital markets and corporate governance in highly regulated and growth-oriented industries.
Charles-Antoine works closely with clients in the life sciences, financial services, and mining and metals sectors, advising both emerging and established organizations at key stages of their development. He regularly represents issuers and underwriters in public offerings, as well as in private placements and other strategic financings. He also counsels Canadian public companies on governance frameworks and ongoing disclosure obligations, helping them navigate regulatory requirements while supporting long-term business objectives.
A trusted advisor to entrepreneurs and management teams, Charles-Antoine supports start-ups and high-growth companies in managing the legal aspects of their expansion, from early-stage financing through to complex transactions. He is frequently involved in significant mergers and acquisitions, acting for buyers of public and private companies, particularly in the context of public takeover transactions.
Charles-Antoine’s representative matters include:
- Advising on the proposed sale of Eddyfi Technologies (a former division of Previan Technologies) to ESAB Corporation for C$2 billion
- Advising iA Financial Corporation on its acquisition of RF Capital Group Inc. for C$597 million
- Advising Aptose Biosciences on its proposed acquisition by Hanmi Pharmaceutical
- Advising Champion Iron Limited on a US$500 million private offering of senior notes
- Advising Feldan Therapeutics on a US$21 million Series B investment round co-led by Genesys Capital and Fonds de solidarité FTQ
- Advising Medicenna Therapeutics on a C$20 million strategic investment by RA Capital Management
In addition to his legal practice, Charles-Antoine serves on the Board of the Québec City Chamber of Commerce and Industry, and is an ambassador for the annual fundraising event of the Orchestre symphonique de Québec.
Charles-Antoine obtained his Bachelor of Laws from Université de Sherbrooke in 2007 and was called to the Québec Bar in 2008.
